# Pleco-Xa Docs Site Implementation Plan

> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development or superpowers:executing-plans. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax.

**Goal:** A deployable Astro Starlight documentation site at `apps/docs/` with an auto-generated per-function API reference (from pleco's JSDoc via TypeDoc), per-namespace narrative guides, an interactive gallery embedding the existing `examples/web` demos, a "Coming from librosa" parity page, and a rewritten README.

**Architecture:** New npm workspace `apps/docs` running Astro + Starlight. `starlight-typedoc` + `typedoc-plugin-markdown` generate the API reference as committed markdown from the library's JSDoc-annotated `.js`. Narrative guides and gallery pages are authored MDX. The site builds statically for Cloudflare Pages.

**Tech Stack:** Astro 5, `@astrojs/starlight`, `starlight-typedoc`, `typedoc`, `typedoc-plugin-markdown`. "Test" per task = the site builds clean and the page renders.

**Spec:** `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-03-pleco-xa-docs-site-design.md`

**Branch:** `docs-site`

---

## File map

```
apps/docs/
├── package.json                    # @pleco-xa/docs workspace
├── astro.config.mjs                # starlight() + starlight-typedoc plugins
├── typedoc.json                    # base typedoc opts (allowJs)
├── tsconfig.json                   # allowJs for TypeDoc
├── src/
│   ├── content/docs/
│   │   ├── index.mdx
│   │   ├── start/{install.md,quickstart.mdx,coming-from-librosa.md}
│   │   ├── guides/*.md             # one per namespace
│   │   ├── gallery/*.mdx           # curated demos
│   │   ├── reference/{parity.md,changelog.md,glossary.md}
│   │   └── api/                    # (generated) typedoc markdown — gitignored or committed
│   └── components/DemoEmbed.astro
└── public/                         # symlink/copy access to demos + audio
```

---

### Task 1: Scaffold the Starlight workspace

**Files:** Create `apps/docs/package.json`, `apps/docs/astro.config.mjs`, `apps/docs/tsconfig.json`, `apps/docs/src/content/docs/index.mdx`; Modify root `package.json`

- [ ] **Step 1: Branch**

```bash
git checkout -b docs-site
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Create `apps/docs/package.json`**

```json
{
  "name": "@pleco-xa/docs",
  "private": true,
  "type": "module",
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "astro dev",
    "build": "astro build",
    "preview": "astro preview",
    "sync-api": "typedoc"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "@astrojs/starlight": "^0.30.0",
    "astro": "^5.15.3",
    "sharp": "^0.33.5",
    "starlight-typedoc": "^0.19.0",
    "typedoc": "^0.28.0",
    "typedoc-plugin-markdown": "^4.3.0"
  }
}
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Create `apps/docs/tsconfig.json`** (lets TypeDoc read the JS)

```json
{
  "compilerOptions": {
    "allowJs": true,
    "checkJs": false,
    "noEmit": true,
    "target": "ES2020",
    "moduleResolution": "bundler",
    "strict": false
  }
}
```

- [ ] **Step 4: Create minimal `apps/docs/astro.config.mjs`** (typedoc wiring added in Task 2)

```javascript
import { defineConfig } from 'astro/config'
import starlight from '@astrojs/starlight'

export default defineConfig({
  site: 'https://plecoxa.com',
  integrations: [
    starlight({
      title: 'Pleco-Xa',
      description: 'Browser-native audio analysis engine — musical timing, spectral features, and intelligent loop detection.',
      social: [{ icon: 'github', label: 'GitHub', href: 'https://github.com/brookcs3/pleco-xa' }],
      sidebar: [
        { label: 'Getting Started', autogenerate: { directory: 'start' } },
        { label: 'Guides', autogenerate: { directory: 'guides' } },
        { label: 'Gallery', autogenerate: { directory: 'gallery' } },
        { label: 'Reference', autogenerate: { directory: 'reference' } },
      ],
    }),
  ],
})
```

- [ ] **Step 5: Create landing `apps/docs/src/content/docs/index.mdx`**

```mdx
---
title: Pleco-Xa
description: Browser-native audio analysis engine.
template: splash
hero:
  tagline: Musical timing, spectral features, and intelligent loop detection — in the browser, with zero dependencies.
  actions:
    - text: Get started
      link: /start/install/
      icon: right-arrow
    - text: Explore the gallery
      link: /gallery/
      variant: minimal
---

Pleco-Xa is a browser-native audio analysis engine. It brings musical
intelligence — BPM and beat tracking, spectral features, structural
segmentation, and its signature loop detection — to any environment that runs
JavaScript, with no build step and no dependencies.

It also, you'll discover, covers everything librosa does — validated function by
function against it — while adding real-time and loop-analysis capabilities that
an offline Python library structurally cannot.
```

- [ ] **Step 6: Add root workspace script.** In root `package.json` `scripts`, add:

```json
    "docs": "npm run dev -w @pleco-xa/docs",
    "docs:build": "npm run build:lib && npm run build -w @pleco-xa/docs",
```

- [ ] **Step 7: Install + verify dev server boots**

```bash
rm -f package-lock.json && npm install
npm run dev -w @pleco-xa/docs &   # or: cd apps/docs && npx astro dev
sleep 6 && curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}\n" http://localhost:4321/
```
Expected: `200`. Kill the dev server.

- [ ] **Step 8: Commit**

```bash
git add apps/docs package.json package-lock.json && git commit -m "Scaffold Astro Starlight docs workspace (apps/docs)"
```

### Task 2: Auto API reference from JSDoc (starlight-typedoc)

**Files:** Create `apps/docs/typedoc.json`; Modify `apps/docs/astro.config.mjs`

- [ ] **Step 1: Create `apps/docs/typedoc.json`** — entry points are the barrel + curated namespace modules. Use the library barrel so all re-exports resolve:

```json
{
  "$schema": "https://typedoc.org/schema.json",
  "entryPoints": ["../../packages/pleco-xa/src/index.js"],
  "tsconfig": "./tsconfig.json",
  "plugin": ["typedoc-plugin-markdown"],
  "readme": "none",
  "skipErrorChecking": true,
  "excludeInternal": true,
  "gitRevision": "main"
}
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Wire `starlight-typedoc` into `astro.config.mjs`.** Replace the integrations array so the plugin generates API pages into `src/content/docs/api/` and injects a sidebar group:

```javascript
import { defineConfig } from 'astro/config'
import starlight from '@astrojs/starlight'
import { createStarlightTypeDocPlugin } from 'starlight-typedoc'

const [starlightTypeDoc, typeDocSidebarGroup] = createStarlightTypeDocPlugin()

export default defineConfig({
  site: 'https://plecoxa.com',
  integrations: [
    starlight({
      title: 'Pleco-Xa',
      description: 'Browser-native audio analysis engine — musical timing, spectral features, and intelligent loop detection.',
      social: [{ icon: 'github', label: 'GitHub', href: 'https://github.com/brookcs3/pleco-xa' }],
      plugins: [
        starlightTypeDoc({
          entryPoints: ['../../packages/pleco-xa/src/index.js'],
          tsconfig: './tsconfig.json',
          typeDoc: { plugin: ['typedoc-plugin-markdown'], skipErrorChecking: true, excludeInternal: true },
          output: 'api',
          sidebar: { label: 'API Reference', collapsed: true },
        }),
      ],
      sidebar: [
        { label: 'Getting Started', autogenerate: { directory: 'start' } },
        { label: 'Guides', autogenerate: { directory: 'guides' } },
        { label: 'Gallery', autogenerate: { directory: 'gallery' } },
        typeDocSidebarGroup,
        { label: 'Reference', autogenerate: { directory: 'reference' } },
      ],
    }),
  ],
})
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Build and verify API pages generate**

```bash
cd apps/docs && npx astro build 2>&1 | tail -15
```
Expected: build succeeds; `ls src/content/docs/api/` shows generated markdown (functions/, and per-namespace groupings). If TypeDoc errors on a specific module, add it to `typedoc.json` `"exclude"` and note it (do NOT silence all errors beyond skipErrorChecking).

- [ ] **Step 4: Verify a sample API page has real content**

```bash
grep -rl "Source:" src/content/docs/api 2>/dev/null | head -1 | xargs head -30
```
Expected: a function page with signature, parameters, returns, and a source link (as validated in the spec's toolchain check).

- [ ] **Step 5: Decide committed vs generated.** Commit the generated `api/` markdown (satisfies the "md version" requirement — the reference exists as readable markdown in the repo). Add a note at `apps/docs/src/content/docs/api/README.md` is auto-managed. Commit:

```bash
cd ../.. && git add apps/docs && git commit -m "Auto-generate per-function API reference from JSDoc (starlight-typedoc)"
```

### Task 3: Getting Started pages

**Files:** Create `apps/docs/src/content/docs/start/install.md`, `.../start/quickstart.mdx`

- [ ] **Step 1: `start/install.md`**

````markdown
---
title: Installation
description: Install Pleco-Xa via npm or load it from a CDN.
---

## npm

```bash
npm install pleco-xa
```

```js
import { detectBPM, loop } from 'pleco-xa'
```

Pleco-Xa is pure ESM with **zero runtime dependencies**. It runs in browsers,
Node, and Web Workers — the analysis API takes `(Float32Array, sampleRate)`, so
the same code works everywhere. Browser helpers for decoding audio live in the
`io` namespace.

## Node

```js
import { decodeWav, feature } from 'pleco-xa'
import { readFileSync } from 'node:fs'
const { channels, sampleRate } = decodeWav(readFileSync('clip.wav').buffer)
const mfcc = feature.mfcc(channels[0], { sr: sampleRate })
```

## Requirements

Node ≥ 18. Any browser with Web Audio, ES modules, and Canvas (Chrome 66+,
Firefox 60+, Safari 14+, Edge 79+).
````

- [ ] **Step 2: `start/quickstart.mdx`** — mirrors librosa's tutorial quickstart (load → tempo → beats → loop), pleco identity:

````mdx
---
title: Quickstart
description: Load audio, detect tempo and beats, and find a loop — the whole pipeline in a few lines.
---

Pleco-Xa's analysis functions take raw samples and a sample rate. In a browser,
decode a file into an `AudioBuffer`, then hand its channel data to the library.

```js
import { beat, loop, io } from 'pleco-xa'

const audioBuffer = await io.load('song.mp3')       // browser: decodeAudioData
const y = audioBuffer.getChannelData(0)
const sr = audioBuffer.sampleRate

// Tempo + beats (librosa-parity Ellis DP tracker)
const { bpm, beats } = beat.beat_track(y, sr)
console.log(`${bpm.toFixed(1)} BPM, ${beats.length} beats`)

// The signature feature: find the best loop
const result = loop.detect(audioBuffer, { strategy: 'fast' })
console.log(`loop ${result.start.toFixed(2)}s → ${result.end.toFixed(2)}s`)
```

Every function on this page is validated against librosa. See
[Coming from librosa](/start/coming-from-librosa/) for the full map.
````

- [ ] **Step 3: Build + verify both render**

```bash
cd apps/docs && npx astro build 2>&1 | tail -5 && cd ../..
```
Expected: build succeeds (broken MDX or bad frontmatter fails the build).

- [ ] **Step 4: Commit**

```bash
git add apps/docs/src/content/docs/start && git commit -m "Docs: getting-started (install + quickstart)"
```

### Task 4: Coming-from-librosa parity page

**Files:** Create `apps/docs/src/content/docs/start/coming-from-librosa.md`; read `packages/pleco-xa/PARITY.md`

- [ ] **Step 1: Generate the fn-map from PARITY.md.** Read `packages/pleco-xa/PARITY.md` (the verified table + exceptions ledger). Author `coming-from-librosa.md` with: an opening paragraph (pleco is its own library; it covers librosa's ground, validated function-by-function; it adds real-time/loop capabilities librosa lacks), a mapping table (librosa call → pleco call → fixture-verified status) built from the PARITY rows, and the exceptions list (display → canvas-native `display` namespace; `icqt`/`griffinlim_cqt` honest throws; audioread codec breadth). No fabricated rows — only what PARITY.md marks verified.

Structure:

````markdown
---
title: Coming from librosa
description: How Pleco-Xa's API maps to librosa, function by function — and where it deliberately differs.
---

Pleco-Xa is its own library, but it covers everything librosa does — validated
function by function against pinned librosa via committed fixtures. If you know
librosa, here is the map.

## Function map

| librosa | pleco-xa | verified |
|---|---|---|
| `librosa.stft` | `stft` | ✓ fixture |
| `librosa.feature.melspectrogram` | `feature.melspectrogram` | ✓ fixture |
| `librosa.feature.mfcc` | `feature.mfcc` | ✓ fixture |
| `librosa.beat.beat_track` | `beat.beat_track` | ✓ exact frames |
| `librosa.pyin` | `pyin` | ✓ grid-exact |
| `librosa.sequence.dtw` | `sequence.dtw` | ✓ bit-exact |
| `librosa.segment.recurrence_matrix` | `segment.recurrenceMatrix` | ✓ |
| ... | ... | ... |

(Complete the table from every verified row in `PARITY.md`.)

## Deliberate differences (exceptions ledger)

- **`librosa.display.*`** → pleco's `display` namespace is canvas-native
  (interactive, in-browser). matplotlib semantics do not apply.
- **`icqt` / `griffinlim_cqt`** → honest not-implemented throws (CQT dual-frame
  inverse); documented, not faked.
- **Node codec breadth** → browser `decodeAudioData` covers most formats; Node
  has built-in WAV plus an injectable decoder hook.

## What librosa can't do

Real-time streaming analyzers (`createRmsMeter`, `createFluxAnalyzer`),
`beat.quickTempo` live estimation, and the `loop` namespace — intelligent
loop-point detection with no librosa equivalent.
````

- [ ] **Step 2: Build + commit**

```bash
cd apps/docs && npx astro build 2>&1 | tail -3 && cd ../..
git add apps/docs/src/content/docs/start/coming-from-librosa.md && git commit -m "Docs: Coming-from-librosa parity page"
```

### Task 5: Per-namespace narrative guides

**Files:** Create `apps/docs/src/content/docs/guides/{core,io,util,beat,onset,feature,filters,effects,decompose,segment,sequence,linalg,cluster,loop,playback,display,play}.md`; read `docs/notes/ship-goal-notes.md` (seed corpus) + each namespace's members

This task is content-heavy and parallelizable across agents (one agent per 3-4 guides). Each guide follows the same template.

- [ ] **Step 1: Guide template (apply to every namespace).** For namespace `X`, create `guides/X.md`:

````markdown
---
title: <Namespace> — <one-line purpose>
description: <what this namespace does>
---

<1-2 paragraph conceptual intro: what problem this solves, how it fits.>

## Key functions

- **`X.fn1(...)`** — <what it does, when to use it>
- **`X.fn2(...)`** — <...>

## Example

```js
import { X } from 'pleco-xa'
// a real, runnable snippet using this namespace's main function
```

## Notes

<gotchas / librosa divergences pulled from docs/notes/ship-goal-notes.md for
this module — e.g. periodic-window convention, Slaney mel default, honest throws.>

See the [API reference](/api/) for every function's full signature.
````

- [ ] **Step 2: `loop.md` is the flagship** — give it the richest treatment: the four strategies (`fast`/`precise`/`musical`/`recurrence`), the confidence convention, why it has no librosa equivalent, and a live loop-detection demo embed (via the `DemoEmbed` component from Task 6). Cross-link the loop gallery pages.

- [ ] **Step 3: Author all 17 guides** using the ship-goal-notes seed corpus for the per-module "Notes" sections. Each guide must name real exported functions (verify against `node -e "import('pleco-xa').then(m=>console.log(Object.keys(m.<ns>)))"`), not invented ones.

- [ ] **Step 4: Build (fails on bad MDX/links) + commit**

```bash
cd apps/docs && npx astro build 2>&1 | tail -5 && cd ../..
git add apps/docs/src/content/docs/guides && git commit -m "Docs: per-namespace narrative guides (loop flagship)"
```

### Task 6: DemoEmbed island + interactive gallery

**Files:** Create `apps/docs/src/components/DemoEmbed.astro`, `apps/docs/src/content/docs/gallery/*.mdx`, `apps/docs/astro.config.mjs` (static-copy the demos+audio)

- [ ] **Step 1: Make the demos + audio reachable from the docs site.** Add to `apps/docs/package.json` a `predev`/`prebuild` that copies the built demos into `public/`:

```json
    "predev": "node scripts/copy-demos.mjs",
    "prebuild": "node scripts/copy-demos.mjs"
```

Create `apps/docs/scripts/copy-demos.mjs` (zero-dep): copy `../../examples/web/*` → `public/demos/`, `../../packages/pleco-xa/dist/pleco-xa.js` → `public/packages/pleco-xa/dist/pleco-xa.js`, and `../../apps/demo/public/audio/*` → `public/apps/demo/public/audio/` — preserving the relative paths the demo `<script>` imports expect (`../../packages/...`). So a demo copied to `public/demos/foo.html` still resolves `../../packages/pleco-xa/dist/pleco-xa.js` from `public/packages/...`.

```js
import { cpSync, mkdirSync } from 'node:fs'
import { fileURLToPath } from 'node:url'
const R = fileURLToPath(new URL('..', import.meta.url))
const pub = R + 'public/'
mkdirSync(pub, { recursive: true })
cpSync(R + '../../examples/web', pub + 'demos', { recursive: true })
cpSync(R + '../../packages/pleco-xa/dist', pub + 'packages/pleco-xa/dist', { recursive: true })
cpSync(R + '../../apps/demo/public/audio', pub + 'apps/demo/public/audio', { recursive: true })
console.log('copied demos + dist + audio into apps/docs/public')
```
Add `apps/docs/public/` to `.gitignore` (generated).

- [ ] **Step 2: `DemoEmbed.astro`** — iframes the copied demo (isolated JS, guaranteed to run):

```astro
---
const { src, height = 640, title } = Astro.props
---
<figure class="demo-embed">
  <iframe src={`/demos/${src}`} title={title} width="100%" height={height} loading="lazy"
    style="border:1px solid var(--sl-color-gray-5);border-radius:8px;background:#111"></iframe>
  <figcaption><a href={`/demos/${src}`} target="_blank">open demo in a new tab ↗</a></figcaption>
</figure>
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Gallery index + curated pages.** Create `gallery/index.mdx` (grid linking the showcased demos) and one `.mdx` per flagship demo:

````mdx
---
title: Vocal separation (real stems)
description: Pure-DSP vocal separation compared against ground-truth stems.
---
import DemoEmbed from '../../../components/DemoEmbed.astro'

REPET-SIM (unsupervised) vs pleco's fingerprint method on a real mix, scored
against the true vocal and instrumental stems. No trained model, no weights — it
runs live below.

<DemoEmbed src="plot-vocal-separation.html" title="Vocal separation" height={900} />
````

Curate ~8-10 flagships (loop detection, vocal separation, laplacian segmentation, pyin, music-sync, spectral harmonics, tempogram, streaming meters, a play-layer choreography). **Dedup the 3 vocal demos here** — showcase `plot-vocal-separation.html` (the Orphans REPET-vs-fingerprint one), link the others under "more demos" or drop them from the gallery.

```bash
curl -s -o /dev/null -w "gallery:%{http_code} demo:%{http_code}\n" http://localhost:4321/gallery/ ; curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}\n" http://localhost:4321/demos/plot-vocal-separation.html
```

## Self-Review Notes

- **Spec coverage:** framework/Starlight ✓ (T1–2), auto API reference ✓ (T2), getting-started ✓ (T3), coming-from-librosa ✓ (T4), per-namespace guides ✓ (T5), interactive gallery ✓ (T6), reference/parity/changelog/glossary ✓ (T7), md-and-website ✓ (T2 commits generated md + site builds), README rewrite ✓ (T8), Cloudflare/CI ✓ (T9), demo dedup ✓ (T6 curation), legacy quarantine ✓ (T8). Strip pass correctly excluded.
- **Placeholder scan:** the guide "Notes"/table-completion steps (T4, T5) describe content to author from named source files (PARITY.md, ship-goal-notes.md, live export lists) — content generation from a named corpus, not a blank "TODO". The API table in T4 shows the format + real verified rows and says "complete from PARITY.md".
- **Consistency:** workspace name `@pleco-xa/docs`, output dir `api`, `DemoEmbed.astro` component, and the copy-demos path scheme are consistent across tasks.
